title: error with podman 1.9.0 and cgroup1
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

podman 1.9.0 fails to run a rootless container with cgroup1, while 1.8.2 is able to run.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Configure a fairly standard cgroup1 setup. Mount output: http://ix.io/2iuO
1. Invoke any run command, e.g. `podman --log-level DEBUG run --rm -it docker://cameronnemo/voidlinux-interactive:latest ash`

**Describe the results you received:**

* Debug log with 1.9.0: http://ix.io/2iuM
* Debug log with 1.8.2: http://ix.io/2iuN

**Describe the results you expected:**

A successful container run, like with 1.8.2.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Happens consistently.

**Output of `podman version`:**

1.9.0:

```
Version:            1.9.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

1.8.2:

```
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

1.9.0:


comments.body:
podman --log-level DEBUG run --rm -it docker://cameronnemo/voidlinux-interactive:latest ash 
Works for me on cgroups V1
$ rpm -q podman
podman-1.9.0-1.fc32.x86_64

@giuseppe Ideas?
One difference may be the management method -- on Void we do not have systemd so it is using the cgroupsfs manager.

are you using exactly the same runc version for both?

Could you share the `config.json` file that is generated (you can get the path from the debug output: `Created OCI spec for container $CTR_ID at $PATH`)?

I believe I have the same issue on Gentoo without Systemd.
The following line in `dmesg` seems informative:
```
cgroup1: Unknown subsys name 'openrc'
```
With a hybrid cgroup setup (probably with legacy v1 as well) I have `/sys/fs/cgroup/openrc`, mounted as
```
openrc on /sys/fs/cgroup/openrc type cgroup (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,release_agent=/lib/rc/sh/cgroup-release-agent.sh,name=openrc)
```
We do not support hybrid cgroups in Podman - that's likely the issue there

> We do not support hybrid cgroups in Podman

I am aware of that, but it works just fine with Podman 1.8.2. I believe it is detected as cgroup v1, and the cgroup v2 mount (`/sys/fs/cgroup/unified/`) is ignored, right?
So, my guess is that a cgroup v1 setup (with openrc) would have the same issue. I did not test that, though.
I did test cgroup v2, but could not get Podman to run either (due to a different issue, probably #5903).

Same runc (same everything) for each version. I am not using the unified hierarchy.

[1.8.2 config.json](http://ix.io/2iYG)

[1.9.0 config.json](http://ix.io/2iYF)

Meanwhile this issue is still opened, how can we downgrade podman to 1.8.2 ?
Following the [install](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation.html) instructions, the [Kubic project](https://build.opensuse.org/project/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable) seems only to provide the latest version 1.9.0.
This appears to be fixed with the 1.9.2 release.
